Q1 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. In nodal analysis, the preferred reference node is a node that is connected to ground.
2. In nodal analysis, the preferred referene node is a node that is connected to many part of the network.
3. The mesh current method works with both planar and non-planar circuit.
4. The mesh current method uses Kirchhoffs voltage law.
Which of the above given statements is/are not correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• We always prefer to take that node as the reference node that is connected to many parts of the network.
• Mesh analysis is valid for planer networks and for its application we apply KVL.

Which of the above statements is/are not correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The transmission line without reflection of voltage and current is called infinite line.

Not attempted

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
The highest power loss in a steam power plant occurs in condenser (about 54%) due to which the maximum efficiency is limited to about 35%.

Q51 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. First-in-first out types of computations are efficiently supported by STACKS.
2. Implementing LISTS on linked lists is more efficient than implementing LISTS on an array for almost all the basic LIST operations.
3. Implementing QUEUES on a circular array is more efficient than implementing QUEUES on a linear array with two indices.
4. Last-in-first-out type of computations are efficiency supported by QUEUES.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Stack is LIFO system so FIFO not supported by stack hence option statement 1 is incorrect and queue is using FIFO so statement 4 is also wrong.

Q53 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Starvation can be avoided by which of the following statements:
1. By using shortest job first resource allocation policy.
2. By using first-come, first serve resource allocation policy.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Starvation can be avoided either by using round-robin policy or FCFS policy, By using SJF starvation is not avoided. Because there may be case that shortest jobs keeps on coming and causing starvation for a large job.

Consider the following statements given below:
1. A compensating winding is embedded in the slots inpole faces that carries armature current, in order to reduce stator field distortion.
2. Interpole in a DC generator must be series excited and should have opposite polarity as that of the next main pole in direction of rotation of the armature.
3. Interpole in a DC motor must be series excited and should have same polarity as that of the next main pole in the direction of rotation of the armature.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Interpole in a DC motor must be series excited and should have polarity opposite to that of the next main pole in the direction of rotation of the armature.

Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
By increasing the brush contact resistance.

Not attempted

The direction of rotation of a shaded pole motor is

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The net flux in the shaded portion of the pole lags the flux in the unshaded portion of the pole resulting in a net torque which causes the rotor to rotate from the unshaded to the shaded portion of the pole.

Q102 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding envelope detector
1. The principle of envelope detection of an AM signal is based on rectifying the incoming signal and passing it through a LPF.
2. Diagonal clipping can occur if detector is not designed properly.
3. Modulation index greater than one is preferable for envelope detection.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
A modulation index of more than 1 results into over modulation which introduces envelope
distortion. So it is not advisable to use modulation index greater than 1.

Solution

(a)
Statement-II is correct explanation for Statement-I.

Q145 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Statement (II): Zero voltage regulation occurs only in leading p.f. loads.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Statement I is wrong.
Negative voltage regulation occurs only in leading p.f. loads.

(d)

Statement (II): The look ahead carry adder speeds up the process by eliminating the ripple carry.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Q148 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Statement (I): For AM modulation, modulation index greater than 1 is recommended.
Statement (II): Envelope detector is used at the receiver end in AM modulation.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Modulation index for AM is kept less than 1. Because envelope distortion occurs at m > 1.

Q149 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Statement (I): An electronic voltmeter reads voltage drop across a very high resistance used in an electronic circuit more accurately than a voltmeter which uses same PMMC instrument as the indicating device used in the electronic voltmeter.
Statement (II): The accuracy of the electronic voltmeter is equal to that of the PMMC instrument used in it.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The accuracy of electronic voltmeter is more than that of the PMMC instrument used in it.
